summary: Gruber highlights Andy Hertzfeld's Folklore.org, a new website documenting the development of the original Macintosh through first-person accounts from the people who built it. He praises Hertzfeld as both a titan of Mac history and a great writer, noting that the site includes contributions from other key figures like Bruce Horn, Susan Kare, and Donn Denman. Gruber frames the site as a gift to the Mac community timed around the Mac's 20th anniversary.
key_points: Andy Hertzfeld launched Folklore.org to preserve first-person stories about the original Macintosh development
The site's software is designed to host stories about multiple projects, though only the Mac history is live so far
Contributors include key original Mac team members like Bruce Horn, Susan Kare, and Donn Denman
money_quotes: Andy Hertzfeld is one of the genuine titans of Mac history.
Not only did he write an enormous chunk of the original toolbox APIs and a ton of innovative Mac software, but, by all accounts, he's also a nice guy.
It's a great web site full of great stories.
It's like a giant 20th-anniversary-of-the-Mac birthday present for the entire Mac community.
key_insight: Andy Hertzfeld's Folklore.org is an invaluable first-person archive of original Macintosh development history, arriving as a fitting 20th-anniversary gift to the Mac community.
